growth_records.cite: Costeira et al. Hemin availability induces coordinated DNA methylation and gene expression changes in Porphyromonas gingivalis. mSystems (2023). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01193-22. [PMC10470040]
growth_records.snippet: These conditions set a fixed dilution rate of 0.1 h-1, which corresponds to constant mean generation time of 6.9 h. Steady state conditions were achieved after approximately 10 mean generations.
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state: specific growth rate mu = dilution rate D = 0.1 h-1 (stated). Doubling time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.1 = 6.93 h (paper reports 6.9 h mean generation time). Hemin-limited condition = BHI supplemented with 0.2 mg/L hemin. Base BHI is a complex/undefined commercial medium; component composition not itemized in paper. pH controlled at 7.0 +/-0.1 (1 M NaOH / 0.5 M HCl), temperature 37 +/-0.3 C, anaerobic (5% CO2/95% N2), 40 rpm. No substrate uptake or product secretion fluxes reported (methylome/transcriptome study).

growth_records.cite: Costeira et al. Hemin availability induces coordinated DNA methylation and gene expression changes in Porphyromonas gingivalis. mSystems (2023). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01193-22. [PMC10470040]
growth_records.snippet: Sterile BHI (supplemented with either 0.2 or 5 mg L-1 hemin for limited and excess conditions... a fixed dilution rate of 0.1 h-1, which corresponds to constant mean generation time of 6.9 h.
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state: mu = D = 0.1 h-1. Doubling time = ln2/0.1 = 6.93 h (paper: 6.9 h). Hemin-excess condition = BHI + 5 mg/L hemin; same operating conditions as limited run (only hemin concentration differs). pH 7.0, 37 C, anaerobic (5% CO2/95% N2), 40 rpm. Base BHI composition not itemized. No uptake/secretion fluxes reported.

growth_records.medium: mucin-serum medium (mucin basal medium + horse serum)
growth_records.cite: Li et al. Nutritional pressure from serum amplifies dysbiotic features in an oral microbiome synthetic community. The ISME Journal (2026). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrag070. [PMC13140499]
growth_records.snippet: The second protocol was based on the experimental growth rate of P. gingivalis in mucin-serum (Td = 15 h), using a D = 0.0462 h-1, and a F = 23.21 ml h-1.
growth_records.notes: Experimental doubling time Td = 15 h for P. gingivalis W83 in mucin-serum medium (with serum; P. gingivalis could not grow in mucin monoculture without serum). growth_rate_per_h = ln2/15 = 0.0462 h-1 (matches the D=0.0462 h-1 the authors set from this value). Monocultures grown anaerobically at 37 C for 7 days, inoculum 1e7 cells/mL, serum tested at 0/5/50% v/v (growth reported in serum-supplemented medium). No substrate/product flux quantified numerically (metabolomics relative only).

growth_records.medium: modified chemically defined medium (mCDM) + 5 mM L-methionine

sec.method: gas chromatography with flame photometric detector
gas chromatography with flame photometric detector

growth_records.cite: Hara, Takeshi et al. Interspecies metabolite transfer fuels the methionine metabolism of Fusobacterium nucleatum to stimulate volatile methyl mercaptan production. mSystems (2024). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00764-23. [PMC10878106]
growth_records.snippet: Porphyromonas gingivalis ATCC33277 ... L-Methionine 14.39 ± 2.93 ... DL-Homocysteine 0.44 ± 0.02 (ppm CH3SH).
growth_records.notes: Table 1 precursor screen: resting cells (OD600=1.0), 5 mM substrate, anaerobic, pH 6.5 (methods default), 16 h. CH3SH from L-methionine = 14.39+/-2.93 ppm, from DL-homocysteine = 0.44+/-0.02 ppm; L-cysteine and L-serine N.D. No net growth (Table S2). Product-secretion (CH3SH) screen only; no substrate uptake rate or biomass normalization provided.

growth_records.medium: 20% heat-inactivated human serum with menadione and hemin
growth_records.cite: Irfan, Muhammad et al. A Porphyromonas gingivalis hypothetical protein controlled by the type I-C CRISPR-Cas system is a novel adhesin important in virulence. mSystems (2024). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01231-23. [PMC10949514]
growth_records.snippet: The deletion of PGN_1547 had no significant impact on growth when the P. gingivalis strains were grown planktonically, with similar doubling times of 9 hours in the case of the wild type and 9.6 hours in the case of the mutant
growth_records.notes: Only growth kinetics reported; NO substrate uptake or product secretion rates measured. Doubling time reported = 9.0 h for wild type. mu calculated: mu=ln2/td=0.693/9.0=0.077 h^-1. Growth medium = 20% heat-inactivated human serum + 1 ug/mL menadione + 5 ug/mL hemin (Fig 3a). No slope value in absolute units given (ANOVA on slopes P=0.7271, ns between strains). Temperature/pH not stated in main text.

growth_records.medium: 20% heat-inactivated human serum with menadione and hemin
growth_records.cite: Irfan, Muhammad et al. A Porphyromonas gingivalis hypothetical protein controlled by the type I-C CRISPR-Cas system is a novel adhesin important in virulence. mSystems (2024). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01231-23. [PMC10949514]
growth_records.snippet: similar doubling times of 9 hours in the case of the wild type and 9.6 hours in the case of the mutant ... no significant differences in the slope values of the linear growth phase (P = 0.7271 by analysis of variance, ANOVA)
growth_records.notes: Delta-pgn_1547 mutant. Only growth kinetics; NO uptake/secretion rates. Doubling time reported = 9.6 h. mu=ln2/td=0.693/9.6=0.0722 h^-1. Growth statistically indistinguishable from wild type (slope ANOVA P=0.7271).

growth_records.cite: Tan KH et al. Porphyromonas gingivalis and Treponema denticola Exhibit Metabolic Symbioses. PLoS Pathogens (2014). DOI:10.1371/journal.ppat.1003955. [PMC3946380]
growth_records.snippet: Three independent continuous mono-cultures of P. gingivalis entered steady state ... with an average absorbance (A650 nm) of 1.86±0.14 equating to 3.9±0.3×10^9 cells mL-1 ... dilution rate of 0.044 h-1 and mean generation time of 15.8 h
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state: mu = dilution rate D = flow/volume = 39 mL/h / 900 mL = 0.0433 h-1, reported as 0.044 h-1. Doubling time = mean generation time reported as 15.75-15.8 h (consistent with ln2/0.044 = 15.75 h). No substrate uptake or product secretion rates measured for this chemostat condition (study focused on transcriptomics and glycine cross-feeding measured separately in batch). pH of OBGM not stated in text; medium composition cited from ref 27 (Veith et al.) not given in article.

growth_records.strain: P. gingivalis W50 + T. denticola ATCC 35405
growth_records.cite: Tan KH et al. Porphyromonas gingivalis and Treponema denticola Exhibit Metabolic Symbioses. PLoS Pathogens (2014). DOI:10.1371/journal.ppat.1003955. [PMC3946380]
growth_records.snippet: There were 6.0±0.7×10^9 P. gingivalis cells mL-1 and 1.0±0.1×10^9 T. denticola cells mL-1 under steady state ... cell densities of both P. gingivalis and T. denticola increased significantly, by 54% and 30% ... in co-culture compared with m
growth_records.notes: Continuous co-culture at same dilution rate D = 0.044 h-1, so steady-state mu = 0.044 h-1 for both partners; doubling time 15.75 h. Co-culture increased P. gingivalis by 54% and T. denticola by 30% in cell number vs mono-culture (both p<0.01). No direct substrate uptake/product secretion rates measured; symbiosis characterized transcriptomically and by glycine cross-feeding (batch experiments). pH and OBGM composition not stated in text.

growth_records.medium: OBGM, OBGM/PBS (1:1), or OBGM/T. denticola conditioned medium (1:1)

sec.method: GC-MS, linear mixed-model regression vs cell number

sec.compound: free glycine (net increase in OBGM/T. denticola conditioned medium)

yields.glycine_production_per_cell: 0.164-0.171 µmole/10^9 cells; total glycine decreased 0.765±0.090 µmole/10^9 cells in OBGM/Td conditioned medium indicating peptide hydrolysis source

growth_records.cite: Tan KH et al. Porphyromonas gingivalis and Treponema denticola Exhibit Metabolic Symbioses. PLoS Pathogens (2014). DOI:10.1371/journal.ppat.1003955. [PMC3946380]
growth_records.snippet: Glycine production by P. gingivalis grown in OBGM/PBS was 0.171±0.012 µmole glycine/10^9 cells which was not statistically different from that determined in OBGM at 0.164±0.020 µmole glycine/10^9 cells ... free glycine increased from 0.75±0
growth_records.notes: Batch cross-feeding experiment: P. gingivalis is net PRODUCER (secretor) of free glycine (opposite of T. denticola which consumes it). Production rates are per-cell yields (µmole/10^9 cells), not per-time specific rates; no gDW conversion possible from data given. T. denticola conditioned medium stimulated glycine production: net +1.51 mM over 46 h (0.75->2.26 mM, p<0.01) vs OBGM/PBS control. Total glycine decreased 0.765 µmole/10^9 cells consistent with peptide hydrolysis as source. Measured by GC-MS. pH not stated; OBGM composition not given.
